screenshot_note: Puppeteer launched but timed out waiting for networkidle2 — same resource-constrained environment as review #29. Screenshots unavailable for this cycle. Code review performed against /workspace/src/ source files. Prior screenshots (qa26–qa29 artifacts on disk) cross-referenced for visual baseline.
carry_open_from_29: Stats Bar 5-column fixed grid (HIGH) — repeat(5, 1fr) unchanged at App.css line 118. Four reviews.
Inter Font not loaded (HIGH) — index.html still has zero font imports. Four reviews.

Mobile Office: worker names hidden at ≤480px (HIGH) — display:none still at Office.css line 1147. A SECOND 480px block at line 2295 ('Luna Review #41') adds font-size:0.32rem to .char__name but never clears display:none, so names remain invisible. The attempted fix is silently inert.
Fetch-Warn/Error Retry buttons missing isSyncing guard (MEDIUM) — lines 1098/1104 still call onClick={syncFromGitHub} with no disabled prop.
Footer quote counter modulo bug (MEDIUM) — (quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1 still the formula at line 1281.
Escape key not clearing selectedTag filter (MEDIUM) — line 766 still only calls document.activeElement?.blur(), does not setSelectedTag(null).
completionToast fully inline-styled (MEDIUM) — all inline style props still present on the toast div at line 1026.
No aria-live region for sync completion (MEDIUM) — lastSynced state announces nothing to screen readers.
Activity log worker filter roster-only (MEDIUM) — roster.map() at line 1245 still the source; guests/contractors not filterable.
App.jsx/Office.jsx monolith (MEDIUM) — Office.jsx grew from 1463 to 1472 lines (+9). App.jsx at 1286 (+1). Combined: 2758 lines across two files.
Activity log 'Last N Events' header shows unfiltered count (MEDIUM) — line 1213: Math.min(activityLog.length, 20) always unfiltered.
Stuck Worker stat card keyboard-inaccessible (MEDIUM) — stat-card--stuck div has onClick but still no tabIndex, no role, no onKeyDown. WCAG 2.1.1 failure.
completionToast aria-live on conditionally rendered element (MEDIUM) — div is conditionally mounted with aria-live already populated; screen readers will miss it.
Clickable task tags have no :focus-visible style (MEDIUM) — .task-tag still absent from App.css focus-visible rules. WCAG 2.4.7 failure.
VIBE_RANK defined twice (LOW) — module-scope VIBE_RANK at line 351 and inline vibeRanking at line 893 are byte-for-byte identical.
Sparkline legend hover-only / no touch fallback (LOW) — no @media (hover: none) in App.css.
polling-paused-pill conditional aria-live (LOW) — still conditionally mounted, aria-live unreliable.
getTaskTags() called twice per worker per render (LOW) — computed at call site AND inside WorkerCard.
Dead CSS 'Luna Review #41' block in App.css (LOW) — still at App.css ~line 1099.
Version string still v1.0.0-chaos (LOW) — thirty reviews.
Worker filter pills no active-clear X affordance (LOW) — inconsistent with task tags which show ' ×' on active.
desk__nameplate--away font-size 0.28rem (LOW) — 4.5px at 16px base; physically unreadable.

suggestions.title: Stats Bar 5-Column Fixed Grid — Carry #27/#28/#29, FOURTH Strike
suggestions.description: App.css line 118 still reads `grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 1fr)`. The StatsBar renders up to 7 cells when both the error-count and stuck-workers cards appear. When they do, the grid silently orphans two cells onto a second row. This has been the single most-requested fix across four consecutive reviews and has received ZERO code changes. One line: change `repeat(5, 1fr)` to `repeat(auto-fill, minmax(160px, 1fr))`. The responsive overrides at 900px (3-col) and 600px (2-col) may then be removed as they become redundant.

suggestions.title: Inter Font Still Not Loaded — Carry #27/#28/#29, FOURTH Strike
suggestions.description: index.html has zero font imports. root CSS declares `font-family: 'Inter', 'Segoe UI', system-ui` (index.css line 20) but Inter is never fetched. Every non-macOS user silently falls back. This has been open since review #27. Fix options (pick one): (A) Add `<link rel='preconnect' href='https://fonts.googleapis.com'>` and `<link href='https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;600;700;800&display=swap' rel='stylesheet'>` to index.html. (B) `npm install @fontsource/inter` and `import '@fontsource/inter'` in main.jsx. Either option is under 5 minutes of work.

suggestions.title: Mobile Worker Names: Broken Fix Attempt in 'Luna Review #41' Block
suggestions.description: Office.css now has TWO `@media (max-width: 480px)` blocks containing `.char__name` rules. The first (line 1133) sets `display: none`. The second (line 2295, comment '=== Mobile Office Scale Fix (Luna Review #41) ===') sets `font-size: 0.32rem` — but never adds `display: block` or `display: revert` to counteract the first rule. Result: names are still invisible at ≤480px. The attempted fix is silently inert because the cascade order means `display: none` from block 1 is never overridden. Additionally, the two blocks conflict on `.office-floor`: block 1 sets `min-height: 0; height: calc(100vw * 0.75)` to produce a fixed aspect-ratio box; block 2 re-introduces `min-height: 65vh`, partially defeating block 1's layout intent. Fix the name issue: in the second block, add `display: block` to `.char__name`. Fix the floor conflict: consolidate both 480px blocks into one, resolving the min-height contradiction.

suggestions.title: NEW: pingTimerRef Not Cleaned on Unmount; Ping Reaction Timer Is a Floating Leak
suggestions.description: Office.jsx `handleCharClick` (line 1051) stores a 700ms `setTimeout` in `pingTimerRef`, but there is no `useEffect(() => () => clearTimeout(pingTimerRef.current), [])` cleanup. More critically, line 1058 fires `setTimeout(() => setPingReaction(null), 3000)` with no ref stored and no cleanup whatsoever. If the Office component unmounts within 3 seconds of a worker being clicked (e.g., navigating to Dashboard tab), React will log a 'setState on unmounted component' warning and the anonymous timer will attempt to execute against the collected component. Fix: add a `pingReactionTimerRef = useRef(null)`, assign the 3000ms timer to it, and add a single unmount cleanup effect: `useEffect(() => () => { clearTimeout(pingTimerRef.current); clearTimeout(pingReactionTimerRef.current) }, [])`. The fix is 4 lines.

suggestions.title: Contradictory 480px CSS Blocks Create Maintenance Minefield — Carry of #3 Above
suggestions.description: Office.css now has three distinct `@media (max-width: 480px)` blocks (lines 1133, 2159, 2296). `.office-floor` receives conflicting rules across blocks 1 and 3. `.char__name` receives conflicting rules across blocks 1 and 3. `.office-wrap` receives conflicting padding from blocks 1 and 3. Any future developer reading Office.css will find it impossible to understand what the effective 480px styles are without mentally resolving three separate cascades. Fix: consolidate all `@media (max-width: 480px)` rules into a single block at the bottom of the file. This is a refactor, not a logic change — no visual differences should result (except the now-fixed display:none issue in suggestion #3 above).

suggestions.title: Fetch-Warn/Error Retry Buttons Missing isSyncing Guard — Carry #28/#29
suggestions.description: Both `fetch-error-banner` (line 1098) and `fetch-warn-banner` (line 1104) Retry buttons call `onClick={syncFromGitHub}` with no `disabled={isSyncing}` prop. The header's `sync-now-btn` at line 1073 correctly disables when isSyncing. A user mashing Retry during a slow network can fire multiple concurrent `Promise.allSettled` calls, each competing to write `setAllWorkers`, `setRoster`, and `setActivityLog`. Race condition: the last settled call wins. One-line fix per button: add `disabled={isSyncing}` to both Retry buttons.

suggestions.title: Stuck Worker Stat Card Keyboard-Inaccessible — Carry #29
suggestions.description: The `stat-card--stuck` div (App.jsx line 454) has an onClick that scrolls to the stuck worker — a genuinely useful interaction — but has no `tabIndex`, no `role`, and no `onKeyDown`. Keyboard-only users cannot reach or activate it. WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1 (Keyboard) failure. Fix: add `tabIndex={0}`, `role='button'`, and an onKeyDown that triggers the same scroll on Enter or Space. Already flagged in review #29 with no movement.

suggestions.title: Clickable Task Tags Have No :focus-visible Style — Carry #29
suggestions.description: When `onTagClick` is provided, task tag spans receive `tabIndex={0}` and `role='button'` (App.jsx lines 295–297). They are keyboard-interactive. But `.task-tag` has no `:focus-visible` rule anywhere in App.css. The defined focus styles are `.worker-card:focus-visible` (line 909) and `.tab-toggle button:focus-visible` (line 915) — task tags are absent. WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) failure. Fix: add `.task-tag:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; }` to App.css.

suggestions.title: completionToast aria-live on Conditionally Rendered Element — Carry #29
suggestions.description: The completionToast div (App.jsx line 1026) is conditionally rendered with `aria-live='polite'` already present when it mounts. Per the ARIA spec, live regions must exist in the DOM before their content changes to reliably trigger announcements. A conditionally-rendered live region is silently ignored by most screen reader/browser combinations. Fix: render a persistent, visually-hidden `<span aria-live='polite' aria-atomic='true' className='sr-only'>` that contains `completionToast ? '🎉 ${completionToast.name} shipped it!' : ''`. The visible toast animation can remain conditional.

suggestions.title: NEW: Ping Feature Has Zero Accessibility Surface
suggestions.description: The new ping interaction (clicking a worker on the Office floor shows a Bloberto one-liner bubble) is purely visual. There is no aria-live announcement when a ping fires, no keyboard way to trigger a ping (workers on the floor are not focusable via keyboard at all — there is no tabIndex on any Character component), and the speech bubble has no role or aria-label. For screen reader users, the entire ping feature is invisible. Minimum fix: add a persistent aria-live='polite' region to the Office that announces ping reactions as they fire. Ideal fix: make Character components keyboard-focusable when onClick is provided.

suggestions.title: Activity Log 'Last N Events' Header Shows Unfiltered Count — Carry #29
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1213: `Last {Math.min(activityLog.length, 20)} events` always reflects the raw activityLog length, ignoring the active filter. When a user applies the 'Errors' filter and sees 2 entries, the header still says 'Last 20 events'. This actively misleads. Fix: derive displayed count from the filtered result — either pass filtered entry count as state or compute `filteredCount` before the JSX return, then render `Last {Math.min(filteredCount, 20)} events` or `{filteredCount} of {activityLog.length} events` when a filter is active.

suggestions.title: NEW: IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS and ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES Defined Inside useEffect Callbacks
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 474 defines `const IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS = { 1: '☕', 2: '💬', 3: '🪟', 4: '🧘' }` inside a useEffect body. Line 490 defines `const ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES = [...]` inside another useEffect. These are constant values — they never change. Defining them inside effects means they are re-evaluated on every effect invocation (i.e., on every variant/wanderIdx change and every variant/worker.status change respectively). Move both to module scope alongside the other constants at the top of Office.jsx. Two-line move, zero risk.

suggestions.title: VIBE_RANK Defined Twice — Carry #29
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 351: `const VIBE_RANK = { 'after-hours': 0, 'slow-day': 1, 'on-fire': 2, 'in-flow': 3, 'crushing': 4 }` at module scope. Line 893: identical `const vibeRanking = { ... }` inside a useEffect. The inline version should simply reference `VIBE_RANK`. DRY violation; if vibe keys ever change, two places need updating.

suggestions.title: Escape Key Not Clearing selectedTag Filter — Carry #29
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 766: `else if (e.key === 'Escape') document.activeElement?.blur()`. Pressing Escape blurs the focused element but does NOT call `setSelectedTag(null)`. If a user activates a tag filter (selectedTag !== null) and presses Escape, the filter remains active even though the visual intent of Escape everywhere in this app is 'clear/cancel'. Fix: `else if (e.key === 'Escape') { document.activeElement?.blur(); setSelectedTag(null) }`.

suggestions.title: App.jsx/Office.jsx Monolith Growing — Carry #28/#29
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: 1463 → 1472 lines (+9 this cycle, +25 since review #27 when extraction was first requested). App.jsx: 1285 → 1286 lines. Combined source: 2758 lines across two files. The new Character features (idle bubbles, error bubbles, ping reactions) were all added as additional useEffect blocks inside the already-massive Character component rather than extracted. Suggested extraction targets remain unchanged from review #27: (1) StatsBar → src/components/StatsBar.jsx, (2) WorkerCard → src/components/WorkerCard.jsx, (3) ActivityLog → src/components/ActivityLog.jsx, (4) Character → src/components/Character.jsx. The Character component alone is now ~580 lines (lines 439–718).

suggestions.title: Footer Quote Counter Modulo Bug — Carry #28/#29
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1281: `(quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1` counts from 1 to BONUS_TAGLINES.length+1 inclusive, but the maximum valid quoteBonus index is BONUS_TAGLINES.length-1. The counter therefore displays positions that will never be reached (e.g., '8/8' for an 8-element array when max index is 7). The array access itself is correct (`quoteBonus % BONUS_TAGLINES.length`), making the counter label inconsistent with the actual cycling behavior. Fix: change the display formula to `(quoteBonus % BONUS_TAGLINES.length) + 1` and the denominator to `BONUS_TAGLINES.length`.

suggestions.title: Worker Filter Pills Have No Active-Clear Affordance — Carry #29
suggestions.description: Activity log worker filter pills (App.jsx line 1248) show an active state (purple background, aria-checked=true) but no ' ×' suffix to hint that clicking will clear the filter. Task tags (line 300) already do `{isActive ? ' ×' : ''}` — the precedent is in the same file. Apply the same pattern: `{member.emoji ?? '🤖'} {member.name}{workerFilter === member.name ? ' ×' : ''}`. One-line change.

suggestions.title: Version String Still v1.0.0-chaos — Carry #28/#29
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1282: `v1.0.0-chaos`. Thirty reviews. The app has shipping features, a real-time sync engine, vibe detection, a rich Office floor, an activity log, keyboard shortcuts, dark/light themes, confetti, and now ping reactions and door events. It is not v1.0.0 of anything. Semantic versioning suggestion: v2.5.0 or v30.0.0-chaos if you want to keep the brand.

suggestions.title: desk__nameplate--away Font Size 0.28rem (4.5px) Is Physically Unreadable — Carry #29
suggestions.description: Office.css line 571: `.desk__nameplate--away { font-size: 0.28rem; }`. At 16px base, 0.28rem = 4.5px. Below sub-pixel resolution on non-retina screens. The '↪ away' label is functionally invisible. Either increase to ≥0.6rem or add `aria-hidden='true'` if decorative. The away sign (`AwaySign` component) handles the semantic communication, so aria-hidden is acceptable.

summary: Review #30 — score drops to 3.5 from 3.9, and every decimal point is earned by features alone. Zero issues fixed from review #29. Zero from review #28. The three high-priority bugs (stats bar grid overflow, missing Inter font, mobile worker name invisibility) now carry their fourth consecutive open status — an achievement that rivals the app's own v1.0.0-chaos version string for stubborn persistence. A 'Luna Review #41' comment block in Office.css reveals that someone attempted to fix the mobile worker name issue by adding `font-size: 0.32rem` to a second @media 480px block, but did not add `display: block` to override the upstream `display: none`. The fix is inert and the names remain invisible — a well-intentioned ghost commit. The same second block re-introduces `min-height: 65vh` on `.office-floor`, directly contradicting the first block's `min-height: 0`, creating a CSS conflict that will confuse any future developer. Accessibility score drops from 3 to 2: the stuck worker card still has onClick without tabIndex/role/onKeyDown (WCAG 2.1.1), task tags still have no :focus-visible (WCAG 2.4.7), and the new ping feature ships with zero accessibility surface — no aria-live, no keyboard trigger, nothing. Code quality drops from 3 to 2: Office.jsx grew another 9 lines, IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS and ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES are defined inside useEffect bodies, and the new ping feature introduces a floating setTimeout with no cleanup ref that will fire setState on an unmounted component whenever a user clicks a worker and then immediately navigates to the Dashboard tab. Performance drops from 4 to 3 on the combination of the leak and the in-effect constant allocations. On the bright side: the new features this cycle are genuinely delightful. Ping reactions with Bloberto one-liners, idle micro-bubbles, error worker distress messages, door event animations — this is first-class UX craft. The visual quality remains a 5. The engineering quality of the surrounding scaffolding is sliding the other direction. The backlog is now 22 items, 4 of which are HIGH, and it has grown by net 2 items this review. At the current rate the codebase will be 3000 lines and 30 unresolved findings before Inter font loads.
